items.title: Pentagon’s ‘Attempt to Cripple’ Anthropic Is Troubling, Judge Says
items.summary: During a hearing Tuesday, a district court judge questioned the Department of Defense’s motivations for labeling the Claude AI developer a supply-chain risk.

items.title: What You Need to Know About the Foreign-Made Router Ban in the US
items.summary: The FCC just banned the sale of new consumer-grade Wi-Fi routers manufactured outside the US. Here’s what it means for you.

items.title: Kentucky woman rejects $26M offer to turn her farm into a data center

items.title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
items.summary: Anthropic’s new auto mode for Claude Code lets AI execute tasks with fewer approvals, reflecting a broader shift toward more autonomous tools that balance speed with safety through built-in safeguards.

items.title: Spotify tests new tool to stop AI slop from being attributed to real artists
items.summary: The idea behind the new tool is to give artists more control over which tracks are associated with their name on Spotify.

items.title: Iran war shows norms of international conflicts have been overturned
items.summary: US threats against energy infrastructure, and Iran's retaliation on its Gulf neighbours, signal a clear change.

items.title: Pilots killed in LaGuardia plane crash named
items.summary: Investigators released details from the final three minutes of cockpit and tower communications, which showed controllers cleared both the plane and a fire truck to cross the runway.

items.title: Russia launches 948 drones at Ukraine in largest attack over 24-hour period
items.summary: Cities across Ukraine were hit, with local officials reporting at least eight people killed and dozens injured.

items.title: Moody's cuts rating on private credit fund run by KKR and Future Standard to junk as bad loans grow
items.summary: The move by Moody's is the latest sign of distress in private credit; retail investors have been rushing to withdraw funds, from Blackstone to Apollo.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: House Republicans pan proposal to split up DHS bill despite openness from Trump
items.summary: House Republicans are pushing back stiffly against the idea of splitting up legislation to fund the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), despite the White House and Senate Republicans saying President Trump is open to doing just that. For weeks, GOP leaders have rejected the idea of funding most of the DHS, including airport screeners and…

items.title: Insulin pills may soon replace daily injections
items.summary: For over a century, scientists have chased the dream of insulin pills, but the digestive system kept destroying the drug before it could work—forcing millions of patients to rely on daily injections. Now, researchers at Kumamoto University have developed a clever workaround using a tiny peptide that helps insulin slip through the intestinal wall.

items.title: Supercomputers just solved a 50-year-old mystery about giant stars
items.summary: Astronomers have finally cracked a decades-old mystery about red giant stars—how material from their deep interiors makes its way to the surface. Using cutting-edge supercomputer simulations, researchers discovered that stellar rotation plays a powerful role in mixing elements across a previously unexplained barrier inside the star.

items.title: New light trap design supercharges atom-thin semiconductors
items.summary: Scientists have found a clever way to supercharge ultra-thin semiconductors by reshaping the space beneath them rather than altering the material itself. By placing a single-atom-thick layer of tungsten disulfide over tiny air cavities carved into a crystal, they created miniature “light traps” that dramatically boost brightness and optical effects—up to 20 times stronger emission and 25 times stronger nonlinear signals. These hollow structures, called Mie voids, concentrate light exactly where the material sits, overcoming a major limitation of atomically thin devices.

items.title: Antimatter traveled by truck for the first time
items.summary: Scientists are envisioning an antimatter delivery program that could ferry antiprotons from CERN to other labs around Europe.

items.title: These insects fly with their legs. Physics explains how
items.summary: Phantom crane flies change the angle of their splayed legs to increase or reduce drag, helping them navigate varying winds.

items.title: Neandertals made antibacterial ointment, but may not have known it
items.summary: A team of scientists re-created the way Neandertals made birch tar and found its antibacterial properties could fight off skin infections.
